frase_original: he fought hard against corruption in all forms, especially with the Canal Ring (every scandal during this time seems to have the word "ring" attached to it, much like every scandal since Watergate has "gate" attached to it.)

texto: Green recorded these initial thoughts about government: "Either the people will pay direct taxes, or they will pay none if they know it; and if they don't know when they pay taxes, it is quite time for the prudent, economical administration of government that they did. The Treasury should just supply the moderate wants of the administration of government; an overflowing treasury brings with it corruption and fraud. It has been the curse of nations, and, profiting by the experience of the past, it becomes the present to avoid the instruments of their overflow.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Michael Rubbinaccio sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Green recorded these initial thoughts about government: "Either the people will pay direct taxes,…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a negocios, government, taxation, útil em momentos de taxation e taxes.
